title: Perspectives on Rehabilitation Using Non-invasive Brain Stimulation Based on Second-Person Neuroscience of Teaching-Learning Interactions.

assets.abstract: Recent advances in second-person neuroscience have allowed the underlying neural mechanisms involved in teaching-learning interactions to be better understood. Teaching is not merely a one-way transfer of information from teacher to student; it is a complex interaction that requires metacognitive and mentalizing skills to understand others' intentions and integrate information regarding oneself and others. Physiotherapy involving therapists instructing patients on how to improve their motor skil

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Intersubjectivity — second-person neuroscience, embodied empathy, shared intentionality, social predictive processing.
